artifacts.claimText: The University of Tartu guidance encourages the use of artificial intelligence, including text robots, in teaching, and frames that use around purposeful, ethical, critical, and transparent practice.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Tartu guidance states that lecturers have the right to decide how artificial intelligence is used in their course and, if necessary, to limit or prohibit its use, with instructions given in the course version information or assignment guidance.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Tartu guidance treats submitting work created partly or entirely with AI under one's own name, without mentioning and describing AI use, as inconsistent with academic integrity and as academic misconduct under the Study Regulations.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Tartu guidance says that if personal data or other sensitive information needs to be processed with AI applications, users logging in with a UT account should use AI application versions approved by the university IT department and ensure the application is on an authorized computer.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Tartu IT wiki states that the university has signed a one-year agreement with OpenAI for ChatGPT Education from 5 December 2025 to 5 December 2026, and that at the current stage licenses are offered primarily to employees.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Claim is narrow and directly supported by the Estonian official guidance; no canonical publication flag is set.
Claim preserves course-level lecturer discretion from the source and does not broaden it beyond the guidance.
Claim uses academic misconduct language because the source explicitly uses akadeemiline petturlus for unacknowledged AI-created work.
Claim is limited to sensitive/personal data processing with AI applications and follows the approved-version condition in the source.
Claim is limited to the IT wiki's license/access statement and does not imply student-wide availability.

artifacts.title: University of Tartu AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: The staged run found a current official University of Tartu guidance page for AI use in teaching and studies and an official UT IT wiki page for ChatGPT Education. The guidance encourages purposeful AI use, allows lecturers to restrict or prohibit AI use in course contexts, treats unacknowledged AI-created work as academic misconduct, and directs sensitive-data AI processing toward UT IT-approved tool versions. The ChatGPT Education page documents a one-year OpenAI agreement and states that licenses are currently offered primarily to employees.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The older 2023 news item and PDF were not used for claims because the live guidance page is current and more direct.
The ChatGPT Education claim is scoped to the official IT wiki access/licensing statement and does not assert general availability to all students.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
